  • SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied alongside increased interest in memory stocks.
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell even after the semiconductor company increased its CPU server market share.

  • AeroVironment (AVAV) stock dropped despite the deployment of its laser technology by the U.S. Army.
  • Apple (AAPL) stock stumbled on reports the iPhone maker might delay the launch of its AI Siri digital assistant.

data.author: William White

data.title: "I Got the NDA…": Lowe's Stock (NYSE:LOW) Gains Despite Garage Door Purchase That Ends With NDA
data.description:
Home improvement giant Lowe's (LOW) is a match for many of its rivals in a lot of ways, but customer service might be starting to lag behind. At least, that is what some might get out of the story of a man who tried to buy a garage door and ended up with a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) out of it. That news did not rankle shareholders, though, as Lowe's shares were up modestly in Thursday morning's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brian McMillan, a 74-year-old retiree from Missouri, has a fondness for making toys for needy children. While he originally made the toys for his grandkids, his grandkids eventually—as grandkids often do—aged out of the toy stage. So McMillan turned his talents toward making toys for local kids, and spent a lot of time working out in his garage. McMillan upgraded his garage to better stand the cold, and bought a new garage door from Lowe's.

The door, sadly, did not arrive quickly. It took so long to arrive that McMillan ended up buying a door elsewhere, and wanted a refund from Lowe's. After several hours of back-and-forth, McMillan was ultimately bumped up to corporate…where corporate demanded he sign an NDA to get his refund. Lowe's clarified that this was not an NDA, but rather, a "release of all claims" form. Area law professor Patrick Perkins, meanwhile, scoffed at the notion, declaring such a form as having the same effect as an NDA. Ultimately, McMillan got his refund, after local news got involved.

Lowe's Own Presidents Day Sale

Meanwhile, Lowe's is also gearing up for Presidents Day on Monday, and has quite a sale prepared, even if it does not actually call the sale a Presidents Day sale. Customers can get access to some impressive buys nonetheless, including 40% off major household appliances.

Multiple breeds of refrigerator are on sale, as are dishwashers, washers and dryers, ovens and more. The best way to find out everything that is for sale during this event is to go in and have a look around. But for those who want to see if Lowe's can compete with, say, Home Depot (HD), there should be some good points to compare across both stores.

Is Lowe's a Buy, Sell or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on LOW stock based on 16 Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 13.61% rally in its share price over the past year, the average LOW price target of $286.55 per share implies 0.91% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Roblox Stock (RBLX) Sinks on Ties to a Double Child Kidnapping Case
data.description:
Roblox (RBLX) stock was down on Thursday alongside growing child safety concerns. The bad news for Roblox today was the video game platform being named among those that led to the kidnapping of two teenage girls. A Nebraska man has been charged with the kidnapping of two sisters, ages 12 and 14, after he initially met them on Roblox. While the conversation moved to Snap's (SNAP) Snapchat after this, Roblox being the initial contact point is a bad look for the company.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Roblox was also named in another case this week, when a man was charged with using the platform, combined with Snapchat and Epic Games' Fortnite, for sexual exploitation of a minor and coercing the production of child sexual abuse material. Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier said, "Parents need to wake up to the disturbing reality that we are seeing online, because yet again, we caught a child predator using Snapchat and Roblox to groom and exploit a child."

The platform has faced increased scrutiny recently, alongside a slew of lawsuits and news stories focused on child predators. This even resulted in Australia launching an investigation into the company's child safety features earlier this week.

Roblox Stock Movement Today

Roblox stock was down 9.1% on Thursday and has fallen 22.88% year-to-date. Despite these drops, the stock has rallied 5.28% over the past 12 months.

RBLX stock movement today saw some 5 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 13.28 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: XRP Price Holds Steady as Ripple CEO Claims that It Has the 'Opportunity to Be a Trillion-Dollar Crypto Company'
data.description:
XRP's price (XRP-USD) is holding steady near $1.38 today, as traders look for a solid bottom after a rocky few weeks. While the value has slipped from recent highs, the mood in the community is far from quiet. Ripple is now moving away from its old image as a speculative coin and is instead focusing on making sure its technology is the go-to choice for the world's biggest banks. CEO Brad Garlinghouse recently told fans at an XRP Community Day that the token is the "North Star" for the company's massive growth plans.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Garlinghouse Targets a Trillion-Dollar Goal

Brad Garlinghouse isn't being shy about where he thinks Ripple is going. "I think Ripple has the opportunity to be a trillion-dollar crypto company," he said during a recent talk. He believes that by following the rules and working with regulators, Ripple has built a moat that other companies can't cross. Even with the market acting shaky lately, XRP ETFs are still seeing $40 million in new money coming in, which shows that the big players are still very interested in the token regardless of the current XRP price.

Aviva Investors Moves Funds On-Chain

The XRP Ledger (XRPL) just landed a huge partner in the UK. Aviva Investors (AVVIY), which manages hundreds of billions of dollars, is working with Ripple to turn traditional investment funds into digital tokens. Nigel Khakoo from Ripple noted that "tokenization is moving from a fun experiment to large-scale production," and the XRPL is being used because it has the security and speed these big firms need.

Zand Bank Expands the Stablecoin Bridge

Ripple is also making big moves in the Middle East through a partnership with Zand, a digital bank in the UAE. Together, they are linking a new dirham-backed stablecoin (AEDZ) with Ripple's own RLUSD. This partnership allows businesses to move money between the U.S. and the UAE almost instantly. The launch of these stablecoins on the XRP Ledger makes it easier for companies to handle cross-border payments without the long wait times or high fees of traditional banks.

Overall, while some traders are upset that the XRP price dropped 20% this month, the long-term fans are pointing at the fact that banks are actually starting to use the technology for trillions of dollars in assets.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.39.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Home Sales Post Largest Monthly Drop in Nearly 4 Years
data.description:
U.S. existing home sales totaled 3.91 million on a seasonally adjusted annual rate in January, falling by 8.4% compared to December and registering the largest monthly decline since February 2022. A severe winter storm that impacted more than two-thirds of the U.S. may have contributed to prospective homebuyers pushing back contract closings.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The below-normal temperatures and above-normal precipitation this January make it harder than usual to assess the underlying driver of the decrease and determine if this month's numbers are an aberration," said National Association of Realtors (NAR) Chief Economist Dr. Lawrence Yun.

Housing Inventory Shortage Keeps Prices Elevated

The slowdown comes despite the NAR's Housing Affordability Index reaching its highest level since March 2022. In addition, wage gains have outpaced home price growth, while mortgage rates have steadily declined since May. The 30-year fixed-rate mortgage currently sits at 6.11%, according to Freddie Mac.

At the same time, the median sales price of homes has remained stubbornly high, while housing inventory is still tight. The median price for a home was $396,800 in January, the highest price for the month on record.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock (LLY) Gets New 'Buy Rating' After Strong Earnings
data.description:
Eli Lilly's stock (LLY) has received a new Buy rating following recent financial results that blew past Wall Street forecasts.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ilya Zubkov, an analyst at Freedom Capital Markets (FRHC), raised his rating on LLY stock to Buy from Hold. He also increased his price target on Eli Lilly's stock to $1,200.00 from $1,050.00, saying he has growing confidence in the company and its prospects.

The Freedom Capital Markets analyst cited Eli Lilly's strong financial results and positive 2026 guidance as key factors behind the upgrade, noting that growth appears to be diversifying beyond the company's highly popular weight-loss drug Zepbound.

Eli Lilly's Technology

Analyst Zubkov also noted Eli Lilly's broad use of technology, saying it is vertically integrated and used to help drive revenue growth at the pharmaceutical company. He added that Eli Lilly's proprietary technology gives it a competitive advantage in the marketplace.

Lastly, Freedom Capital emphasized that Eli Lilly's technology makes customers less likely to switch providers and potentially supporting stronger valuation multiples for the stock over the long-term. LLY stock has gained 20% over the past year.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Private AV Startups are About to Take Over U.S. Streets—Here's How Cities Will Change in 2026
data.description:
On Feb. 11, 2026, Morgan Stanley (MS) released a research report calling 2026 an inflection year for private autonomous vehicle (AV) companies. The firm said privately backed operators are expanding into additional U.S. cities and accelerating commercial deployment.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Private AV Companies Expand Across U.S. Cities

According to Morgan Stanley Research, autonomous vehicle services, largely operated by private companies, are now active in eight major U.S. cities after more than two decades of development. The firm expects acceleration in 2026, driven mainly by private AV operators, with 33 consumer launches and expansion into nine additional cities. "2026 is set to be a year for inflection," said Brian Nowak, Head of U.S. Internet Research at Morgan Stanley. 

Morgan Stanley estimates AV miles driven in the U.S. reached 116 million in 2025 and will grow at an average annual rate of 103% through 2032, reaching about 16 billion miles. Even then, AV miles would account for roughly 0.5% of total U.S. miles driven and about 30% of rideshare miles. For investors, Nowak has said that many will closely monitor those metrics to see which companies are moving fastest. He noted that both private and public players, including vehicle manufacturers, sensor and camera makers, software developers, and rideshare companies, are scaling production and forming partnerships.

Private Companies Firms Expand Beyond AVs

Morgan Stanley's projected acceleration in the deployment of self-driving cars is already reflected in funding activity among private AV startups. Waabi, the U.S.-based autonomous trucking company, recently secured $750 million to commercialize its self-driving technology and an additional $250 million in milestone funding from Uber (UBER) to expand into robotaxis. Separately, WeRide (WRD) and Uber launched their first commercial robotaxi service in downtown Abu Dhabi, advancing the scale of urban deployment by private AV operators.

For investors interested, Morgan Stanley Research also identified sectors where private companies are driving transformative change beyond AVs. The firm cited drone technology, operational technology, cybersecurity, AI-powered healthcare tools, and space-based infrastructure as areas gaining momentum. The report added that private capital remains elevated, with $4.3 trillion in dry powder at the end of 2026, and that 2025 take-private activity ranked second-highest in the past decade.

data.author: Joy Iyke
